ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic hamburger entry (FOODON_00002383); no specific entry exists for a double beef burger feast meal with fries, drink, and pie. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers hamburger as a generic category. No specific code exists for a double-patty spicy tomato variant feast meal. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for this fast-food burger feast combination.

serving.description: 1 feast meal (burger + M fries + drink + pie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard feast serving comprises the double beef burger, a medium portion of french fries, a regular soft drink, and a pie, estimated at approximately 680 g total edible weight.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Double Beef Burger Feast — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product (cr_id G00010120). All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous fast-food double beef burger data (USDA SR Legacy #21110) and documented below.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous fast-food hamburger bun and mixed burger meal estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. GI estimated at 66 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a fast-food hamburger (white bun + beef patty composite), which reports GI ~66 (medium). The feast format includes deep-fried fries (GI ~63–75) and a sugary beverage (GI ~63), consistent with a medium-to-high composite GI. A conservative medium GI of 66 is applied to the per-100g composite. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (66 × 195) / 100 ≈ 128.7, where carbohydrate per 680g serving ≈ 195g (29.57 × 6.8).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (487 mg/100g) with low potassium-to-sodium ratio and minimal dietary fibre offset across the full feast
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal; if consumed, request no added salt on fries, substitute the sugary beverage with water, and limit portion to half the burger only, as the full feast can deliver over 3,300 mg sodium — well above the 2,000 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (29.57 g/100g, GI ~66) from white bun, deep-fried fries, and likely sugary beverage, with low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g) providing minimal glycaemic buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal in its full feast format; if the burger alone is consumed, replace the sugary drink with water or unsweetened tea, skip the fries, and monitor post-prandial blood glucose closely given the medium-to-high glycaemic index and high carbohydrate density.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.8 g/100g), trans fat (~0.3 g/100g), high sodium (487 mg/100g), and cholesterol (29 mg/100g) in an ultra-processed fast-food meal format
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal; the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and refined carbohydrates from fries and bun collectively elevates LDL cholesterol, blood pressure, and inflammatory markers — all established CVD risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Double beef patties (high arachidonic acid), probable trans fat (0.3 g/100g), nightshade-based spicy tomato sauce, and deep-fried fries generating advanced glycation end-products (AGEs)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal; the pro-inflammatory combination of red meat, trans fat, nightshade sauce, and AGE-rich deep-fried fries can exacerbate joint inflammation and disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Double Beef Burger Feast is a fast-food set meal sold in Singapore, consisting of a double beef patty burger topped with spicy tomato sauce served in a refined white wheat bun, accompanied by a medium portion of deep-fried french fries, a regular soft drink, and a pie. The burger patties are grilled beef, delivering a savoury, slightly spicy flavour profile from the tomato-chilli sauce, with a soft bun texture contrasting the crispy fries. As an ultra-processed fast-food feast, it is calorie-dense at 263 kcal/100g, with moderate protein (13.3 g/100g), moderate fat (10.18 g/100g including saturated and trans fats), and high refined carbohydrates (29.57 g/100g) from the bun and fries. Sodium is notably high at 487 mg/100g, making the full feast a significant sodium load. Dietary fibre is low at 1.8 g/100g. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 66 (medium), but the glycaemic load of the full feast is very high due to the large carbohydrate volume from fries, bun, and sugary beverage. Clinically, this meal is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is best consumed occasionally and with modifications such as substituting the sugary drink with water and limiting fries intake. The meal is typical of Western-style fast food adapted for the Singapore market.

confidence_reason: Core per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010120, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous fast-food double beef burger data and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous fast-food hamburger entry with full documentation.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.8 g/100g) is not reported in the HPB source for this entry; it has been estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #21110 (fast-food hamburger, double patty with condiments) which reports ~3.8 g saturated fat per 100g for a comparable product. This is documented as an adapted value. 2. Trans fat (0.3 g/100g) is referenced in the input health_suitability notes; this value is consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for fast-food double beef burgers and is retained as an estimated value. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific Singapore fast-food feast product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a matched composite entry as of 2026. 4. Serving size of 680g is an estimate based on typical fast-food feast meal weights (burger ~220g + fries ~115g + drink ~300g + pie ~45g); no official declared weight was available in the HPB source. 5. Halal status is assumed based on the Singapore fast-food market context; official halal certification should be verified with the restaurant operator. 6. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database fast-food hamburger entry; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources.
